description: Database-owned bilingual style taxonomy. Broad group IDs remain stable; normalized child style IDs unify equivalent genre names used by different music platforms.
scope: Normalization target for all platform artist tags. Platform labels are aliases only and never become database identifiers.

source_types.platform_alias: A label retrieved from a platform genre or style vocabulary and converted to one database standard style.
source_types.standard_alias: A language or spelling variant used for searching the same database standard style.
source_types.curated_family_assignment: Our parent-family placement for a cross-platform style; platform category trees can differ.

rules: NN identifies one broad genre family; NN.MM identifies one normalized child style within that family.
The first two characters of every standard_style_id must equal its parent standard_group_id.
Platform-specific terms are stored in platform_aliases and resolve to the same standard_style_id.
IDs are strings so leading zeros remain stable in JSON, database storage, and API queries.
The 90 namespace is reserved for non-genre auxiliary tags and is not included in genre-family expansion.

query_semantics.group_query: Selecting a standard_group_id matches every standard_style_id beginning with standard_group_id plus '.'.
query_semantics.style_query: Selecting a standard_style_id matches that child style only and never automatically adds its parent.
query_semantics.platform_alias_query: Resolve a platform label to its standard_style_id first, then apply exact style-query behavior.
query_semantics.auxiliary_query: Match standard_auxiliary_id exactly; auxiliary IDs never expand into genre groups.

selection_policy: Keep a child style when it appears in a retrieved platform vocabulary or is needed to unify directly equivalent platform terms.
Prefer stable genre/style concepts over language, mood, scene, era, instrument, or editorial playlist tags.
Keep regional pop styles used as genre labels by Apple Music because they are useful for the Chinese, Japanese, and Korean artist tables.
Do not infer QQ Fsinger_tag numeric meanings from this taxonomy; raw platform-ID mapping remains a separate evidence-based process.

aliases: クラシック・クロスオーバー
インストゥルメンタル
TV サウンドトラック

auxiliary_tags.reason: Artist role or practice rather than genre.
Audience or usage category rather than genre family.
Spoken-performance category rather than music style.
Scene and production context; keep as auxiliary unless the source specifies a concrete style such as indie rock.
Vocal synthesis scene and production context rather than a strict musical genre, but useful for artist and song tagging.
